package.name: @igrp/framework-next-auth
package.description: NextAuth/Auth.js wrappers, OIDC, session, JWT, middleware. The root of the framework dependency chain.

persona.mindset: Treat every change as a potential security boundary. When the user works in this package, mentally walk the full auth flow and look for token leaks, missing validations, and edge-runtime violations before approving anything.

topics.topic: edge-runtime constraints: no Node APIs, limited crypto, no dynamic require
interaction with route handlers + Server Components
keeping server-only code out of client bundles
why tsup is chosen here (no React Compiler needed for auth logic)

public-entry-points.note: Consumers import from explicit subpaths, not dist/.
public-entry-points.rule: Don't collapse these subpaths — they keep server code out of client bundles. New symbols go behind the right entry point, and export from that entry's barrel, not the root.

build.after-type-change: Run pnpm build:framework — @igrp/framework-next-types re-exports types from here.

review-checklist.item: Client bundle safety — no server-only imports leaking through ./client.
Audience + issuer validation on every JWT verification.
Refresh-token rotation — old refresh token is invalidated on use.
Session lifetime bounded — no unbounded sessions; reasonable maxAge.
PKCE + state + nonce correct in the authorization-code flow.
Middleware stays edge-safe — no Node-only APIs, no dynamic require.
Cookies — httpOnly, secure, sameSite set correctly per environment.
No tokens logged — redact in every log path.
Registry/tag unchanged — publish still targets internal Sonatype with --tag latest.

coworker-guide.auth-flow-to-walk-mentally:
client → /login → NextAuth signIn → provider auth endpoint → callback URL → code + state → NextAuth callback → token endpoint (exchange + PKCE) → jwt callback (populate token) → session callback (populate session) → encrypted session cookie → middleware validates on next request → refresh when token near expiry (rotate refresh token) → signOut → end-session endpoint → clear cookie
On any change, ask: which step does this touch, and what's the blast radius downstream?

common-pitfalls.pitfall: Importing ./server from a client component — breaks the bundle.
Forgetting trustHost: true when deployed behind a proxy.
Setting strategy: "database" without implementing an adapter.
Using a Node-only crypto primitive in middleware (fails at runtime in edge).
Hardcoding secret or OIDC clientSecret in client code.
Missing rotation on refresh — lets a stolen refresh token live forever.
